en.text: O best of the valiant
(such seers) are to be known as
which enter the mind
is able to attain to
by ignorance – by the Lord’s external potency (avidyā-śakti), to fulfill the living being’s desire to enjoy the material world
sees the illumination within
experiences the joy of the self within
who delights in the self within
athough engaged in the functions of
and even by the senses
The person with mind detached
devoid of the delusion of considering one’s body and its attachments as ‘me’ and ‘mine’
as the maintainer, or worshippable objective
attaining the transcendental state
liberation from the mundane plane
one in knowledge of Brahma
absorbing himself in meditation on the Absolute
unto the Supreme Lord
by the intelligence
external sense objects of sound, etc
The childish, the unwise
the cause of sorrow
of the two
grasping objects in the hands
in relation to their respective sense objects of form, etc
who has mastery over the senses
it is to be known that
the fruits of action
to renunciation of action
the path of selfless action
ego of considering themselves ‘the doer’
the impulses of desire and anger
and free from desire and anger
by the body
by the mind
dedicated to liberation
are deluded, thinking the body to be the self
a contemplative seeker of the self
being a man of knowledge
nor their association with the fruits of actions
nor causing work for others
neither the sin
in the city of nine gates
and closing the eyes, or blinking
and being faultless (free from attachment and hatred)
one who is free from duality
flowing through the nostrils
to the fruits of action
inhalation and exhalation
Those who see with equanimity, who see Brahma, transcendence
renunciation of action and the path of selfless action
the cycle of birth and death
dedicated to the welfare of all
having reached the state in which his whole being is pervaded with affection and compassion for all beings
the worshippable Supreme Lord of all planes Lord Nārāyaṇ
such a (niṣkām karma-) yogī (follower of the yoga of selfless action)
born of the senses contacting their objects
attachment (to the fruits of action)
endowed with steady intelligence
and the benefactor of all living beings by mercifully teaching pure devotion to Me, through My devotees, since I am the adorable friend of the devotees Lord Kṛṣṇa
nor the peity
the paths of renunciation and (karma-) yoga
both sannyās and karma-yoga
who think constantly of the Supreme Lord
and who are devoted to hearing and singing His glories
are the origin of misery
who are knowers of the element tvam, the soul
in a learned and humble
free from desire, fear, and anger
of the karmīs’ sacrifices and the jñānīs’ austerities
which is experienced in self-realisation
who have controlled their thoughts (conquered the subtle body)
is a controller of the senses, mind, and intelligence
Of the sannyāsīs
of those for whom
A niṣkām karma-yogī, one engaged in the yoga of selfless action
they begin and end
like the sun that dispels darkness
for purification of the heart, self-purification
by self-knowledge, by the Lord’s superior potency
is able (by the yoga of selfless action)
leaving the body
the joy of his original divine identity

ru.text: путь философского познания духа и материи
благодаря са̄н̇кхйа-йоге, пути философского познания духа и материи
рождены из взаимодействия чувств с их объектами
твердо убежденные в истинности пути служения Кришне
